2010.text: Over the last decade, Silicon Valley has transformed every individual with access to the Internet into a researcher. It used to be that you needed a Masters degree or a Ph.D. in a particular field, ties to highly specialized experts and tomes buried in dusty university libraries, to meet the myriad intellectual demands of work and life. Now — with a little help from Google and Wikipedia – anyone c
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information. We were founded in 2004 by a handful of PayPal alumni and Stanford computer scientists. Since then we’ve doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic, and rigorous hiring standards. We currently offer two products: Palantir Government and Palantir Finance
2010.heading: About Palantir

2010.text: Palantir was built by technologists serious about protecting privacy and civil liberties. Thoughts on Palantir and the promise of analysis by our CEO, Alex Karp. Read » The Palantir White Papers. Learn more about the science behind our technology. Learn about Palantir with Operation Tradestop , an interactive analysis game leveraging Palantir Government.
2010.heading: Palantir’s Commitment to Protecting Civil Liberties

2010.text: Palantir places all the analytical power and technical prowess of the world’s best software engineers at your fingertips. Palantir shifts the paradigm away from both the etching technologies that merely let you map out what you already know and the quasi stand-alone field of artificial intelligence, that while useful for some types of analysis, has limits when it comes to making sense out of massi
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: At Palantir, we are only hiring the best and the brightest computer engineers to help us harness this awesome power to meet today’s vast analytical needs. As the CEO and founder of Palantir Technologies, I can say with pride that every engineer on the Palantir team is working with a sense of excitement and mission, convinced that we have created a product that will help analysts take quantum leaps
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2013.heading: Palantir

2013.text: On November 13, Pooja Sankar, CEO of Piazza, an online Q&A platform used by students and teachers, sat down with four women at Palantir to discuss their experiences working in technology. Originally broadcasted live to students in Piazza’s network, the panelists spoke about their roles at Palantir and how they’ve navigated the ups and downs [...]
2013.heading: Panel features Palantir Women on Working in Tech

2013.text: Last month we hosted Palantir Sync: Investigate, which brought together members of the investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities for a discussion of how technology and data are transforming their missions. Through a series of presentations we demonstrated how our software could provide a single, end-to-end platform for their investigations—from initial lead all the way to [...]
2013.heading: Palantir Sync brings together investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities to discuss how technology can revolutionize their missions

2013.heading: Follow us elsewhere

2013.text: Girl Geeks from around the Bay rang in the new year and broke in Palantir’s new event space with Girl Geek Dinner #32, which focused on philanthropy, technology, and the opportunities presented by both for women worldwide. Lucy Bernholz, visiting scholar at Stanford University’s Center on Philanthropy and Civil Society, opened the night with her [...]
2013.heading: Palantir hosts Bay Area Girl Geek Dinner #32

2014.text: At the heart of what we do is a desire to connect people with the information they need to achieve extraordinary outcomes. Our solutions and integrations are revolutionizing the work being done at organizations across government, commercial and health industries. We build groundbreaking technologies , including our data fusion platforms . Protecting civil liberties . Promoting open software . Surf
2014.heading: Palantir

2015.text: Perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities: we believe people are our most important asset, so we invest in our people every day to promote productivity and reduce friction.
2015.heading: Life at Palantir

2015.text: With a combination of perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities, we invest every day in our most important asset: our people.
2015.heading: Life At Palantir

2015.text: Where you take the next step in your career matters. While we certainly think we’re doing exciting, impactful work, we don’t want you to just take our word for it. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
2015.heading: Get to Know Us Better

2015.text: Our environment is designed to make work energizing and to stimulate the best thinking. We have no set hours—as long as your work is getting done, you're free to work whatever schedule suits you. Some folks come in during "normal business" hours and head home for dinner with their families; others spend 16 hours a day at the office—not because they're working that whole time, but because the offic
2015.heading: Choose Your Own Adventure

2015.text: Engineers build to solve problems, but at Palantir you don't have to be a computer scientist to be an engineer. You do have to speak up when things aren't right and build to fix what's broken.
2015.heading: Engineering Culture

2016.heading: Choose Your Own Adventure

2016.text: At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world. We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life. Palantir interns apply their knowledge to real-world problems from day one. Learn more.
2016.heading: There is so much left to build

2016.text: Focus on what matters: Palantir provides a variety of perks that help us focus on the mission by promoting productivity and freeing up time at and away from the office. Good grub: At Palantir, you will never be hungry. Chefs craft weekly menus consisting of organic and local foods, and we serve three meals a day. Family, friends, and guests are always welcome, and to-go boxes are always available.
2016.heading: Making Life Easy

2017.text: We are engineers on a mission. We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms
2017.heading: We go where we’re needed most

2017.text: We believe in augmenting human intelligence, not replacing it. With good data and the right technology, people and institutions today can still solve hard problems and change the world for the better. In 2004, when we looked at the available technology, we saw products that were too rigid to handle novel problems, and custom systems that took too long to deploy and required too many services to ma
2017.heading: Why We’re Here

2017.text: We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line. To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machin
2017.heading: We make products for human-driven analysis of real-world data

2017.text: We’re engineers, not academics. At our headquarters in Palo Alto and at offices around the world we’ve assembled a team that combines practical expertise in distributed systems infrastructure, big data processing, user experience design, and data science. Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mis
2017.heading: We build our company around mission-driven engineering

2017.text: Our customers have data and a deep understanding of the problems they face. We have proven products and an engineering mindset. We send our engineers into the field to work directly with our customers—deploying our products, integrating their data, optimizing their workflows, and producing operational results in weeks, not years. By establishing a true partnership, we help customers get the most o
2017.heading: We meet the problems where they live

If you want to stare into the face of important problems and have the freedom to solve them, we want to work with you. We have some resources to help you navigate the hiring process.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.

If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.

2025.text: If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.

At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world. We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life.
We build software that empowers organizations to effectively integrate their data, decisions, and operations.
2025.heading: Palantir

2025.text: Our customers have data and a deep understanding of the problems they face. We have proven products and an engineering mindset.

2026.text: We attract people who are intellectually diverse and contrarian at heart. Palantirians have strong convictions and open minds. Whether you have PhD or GED, deep thinking matters most.
2026.heading: Free Thinkers

2026.text: We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins. On the factory floor. In the operating room. Across the battlefield. We build with consequence.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.
We build software that empowers organizations to effectively integrate their data, decisions, and operations.

Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mis
2017.heading: We build our company around mission-driven engineering

2017.text: We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line. To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machin
2017.heading: We make products for human-driven analysis of real-world data

2017.text: We believe in augmenting human intelligence, not replacing it. With good data and the right technology, people and institutions today can still solve hard problems and change the world for the better. In 2004, when we looked at the available technology, we saw products that were too rigid to handle novel problems, and custom systems that took too long to deploy and required too many services to ma
2017.heading: Why We’re Here

2017.text: Our customers have data and a deep understanding of the problems they face. We have proven products and an engineering mindset. We send our engineers into the field to work directly with our customers—deploying our products, integrating their data, optimizing their workflows, and producing operational results in weeks, not years. By establishing a true partnership, we help customers get the most o
2017.heading: We meet the problems where they live

2018.text: We are engineers on a mission. We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms

2021.text: No matter where you're based, or which team you work on, you’ll be part of a group of people working together to build solutions to mission-critical problems and a company that values the very best ideas.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.

We are engineers on a mission. We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms
2021.heading: We go where we’re needed most

2021.text: We’re engineers, not academics. At our offices around the world, we’ve assembled a team that combines practical expertise in distributed systems infrastructure, big data processing, user experience design, and data science. Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mission.
2021.heading: We build our company around mission-driven engineering

2021.text: Engineers build to solve problems, but at Palantir you don't have to be a computer scientist to be an engineer. You do have to speak up when things aren't right and build to fix what's broken.
2021.heading: Engineering Culture

2022.text: No matter where you're based, or which team you work on, you’ll be part of a group of people working together to build solutions to mission-critical problems and a company that values the very best ideas.
If you want to stare into the face of important problems and have the freedom to solve them, we want to work with you. We have some resources to help you navigate the hiring process.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.
There’s a role here for you—whether you're a new grad or have years of experience.

We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursui

Where you take the next step in your career matters. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
There’s a role here for you—whether you're a new grad or have years of experience.

At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and within commercial and governmental institutions.
If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.

We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursui

2025.text: If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.

We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursui

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.
Our customers have data and a deep understanding of the problems they face. We have proven products and an engineering mindset.

At our offices around the world, we’ve assembled a team that combines practical expertise in distributed systems infrastructure, big data processing, user experience design, and data science. Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mission.

2026.text: We attract people who are intellectually diverse and contrarian at heart. Palantirians have strong convictions and open minds. Whether you have PhD or GED, deep thinking matters most.
2026.heading: Free Thinkers

2026.text: Palantirians make decisions quickly. We optimize for impact, not consensus. There is no bureaucratic distance between identifying a problem and fixing it.
2026.heading: Fast-movers

2026.text: You are not evaluated by distant executives — you are judged by outcomes. There's no oversight from anyone who isn't directly in the problem with you. Your work will speak for itself.
2026.heading: Independents

2026.text: Palantirians take initiative. There's no top-down instruction holding you back. This creates unusual freedom: when you see a solution, you own it.
2026.heading: Catalysts

2026.text: We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins. On the factory floor. In the operating room. Across the battlefield. We build with consequence.

stanceMaxQuote.text: Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information. We were founded in 2004 by a handful of PayPal alumni and Stanford computer scientists. Since then we’ve doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic, and rigorous hiring standards. We currently offer two products: Palantir Government and Palantir Finance
stanceMaxQuote.heading: About Palantir

stanceMinQuote.text: Over the last decade, Silicon Valley has transformed every individual with access to the Internet into a researcher. It used to be that you needed a Masters degree or a Ph.D. in a particular field, ties to highly specialized experts and tomes buried in dusty university libraries, to meet the myriad intellectual demands of work and life. stanceMaxQuote.text: With a combination of perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities, we invest every day in our most important asset: our people.
stanceMaxQuote.heading: Life At Palantir

stanceMinQuote.text: Our environment is designed to make work energizing and to stimulate the best thinking. We have no set hours—as long as your work is getting done, you're free to work whatever schedule suits you. Some folks come in during "normal business" hours and head home for dinner with their families; others spend 16 hours a day at the office—not because they're working that whole time, but because the offic
stanceMinQuote.heading: Choose Your Own Adventure

stanceMaxQuote.text: Analytic technology, especially in the hands of powerful institutions that hold large volumes of data, can pose serious risks to privacy and civil liberties. That's why we build privacy-protective capabilities into our products, help customers understand how to use them responsibly, and work with advocacy groups and the policy community on how technology can be used to protect privacy interests to
stanceMaxQuote.heading: We design technology to help institutions protect liberty
